title: The Bias Fingerprint: Eight Methods Disagree in One of Seven Cochrane Meta-Analyses
author: Mahmood Ahmad
body: When eight publication-bias methods are applied to the same meta-analysis, how often do they point to the same conclusion? We applied four detection tests and four correction methods to 307 Cochrane reviews with at least five studies from the Pairwise70 dataset. Each review was assigned a bias fingerprint summarising which tests flagged bias and how the correction methods shifted the pooled estimate, then classified as Clean, Suspected, Confirmed, or Discordant. Only 54 reviews (17.6%) were Clean, while 42 were Discordant, giving a discordance rate of 13.7% and a median risk-ratio shift below 0.05 (95% CI 0.02 to 0.08). Agreement ranged from 98% between PET-PEESE and limit meta-analysis to 73% between trim-and-fill and regression-based corrections, with a mean relative effect shift of 1.01. In roughly one in seven Cochrane reviews, the apparent direction of the pooled conclusion depends on which bias method is chosen. These methods detect statistical asymmetry rather than bias itself and cannot fully separate publication bias from clinical or methodological heterogeneity.

sentences.text: When eight publication-bias methods are applied to the same meta-analysis, how often do they point to the same conclusion?
We applied four detection tests and four correction methods to 307 Cochrane reviews with at least five studies from the Pairwise70 dataset.

Each review was assigned a bias fingerprint summarising which tests flagged bias and how the correction methods shifted the pooled estimate, then classified as Clean, Suspected, Confirmed, or Discordant.
Only 54 reviews (17.6%) were Clean, while 42 were Discordant, giving a discordance rate of 13.7% and a median risk-ratio shift below 0.05 (95% CI 0.02 to 0.08).
Agreement ranged from 98% between PET-PEESE and limit meta-analysis to 73% between trim-and-fill and regression-based corrections, with a mean relative effect shift of 1.01.
In roughly one in seven Cochrane reviews, the apparent direction of the pooled conclusion depends on which bias method is chosen.
These methods detect statistical asymmetry rather than bias itself and cannot fully separate publication bias from clinical or methodological heterogeneity.

checks.name: single paragraph
checks.detail: Body must not contain blank-line paragraph breaks.

checks.name: no headings
checks.detail: No markdown headings allowed in body.

checks.name: no links
checks.detail: No links or DOI links allowed in body.

checks.name: result sentence has interval
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should include a confidence interval.

checks.name: result sentence has estimand
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should name an effect measure or test metric.

checks.name: boundary sentence present
checks.detail: Sentence 7 should express a limitation, harm, or scope boundary.
